changed.from: For every (m,a,b,r,kappa) satisfying the assumptions, orbit-averaging over permutations within A_m and within B_m maps each elliptope point X with diag(X)=1 to a point in E_m^blk with weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}, and maps each law P in P_m^bal to a law in P_m^sym with the same one-point margins and weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}(X(P)). Consequently inf_{X : X is positive semidefinite and diag(X)=1} F_{r,kappa}(X) = inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X) and inf_{P in P_m^bal} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) = inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)).
changed.to: For every (m,a,b,r,kappa) satisfying the assumptions, orbit-averaging over the full two-block automorphism group generated by permutations within A_m, permutations within B_m, and the swap of the two equal communities maps each elliptope point X with diag(X)=1 to a point in E_m^blk with weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}. The same averaging maps each law P in P_m^bal to a block-exchangeable law with the same zero one-point margins and weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}(X(P)). Consequently inf_{X : X is positive semidefinite and diag(X)=1} F_{r,kappa}(X) = inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X) and inf_{P in P_m^bal} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) = inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)).
changed.reason: Averaging only over permutations within A_m and within B_m leaves two separate within-block second moments and need not land in the two-parameter slice X(u,v). Adding the community swap is the minimal symmetry correction; the graph and objective are invariant under it because the communities have equal size and the same within/cross weights.

changed.from: There exist explicit frontiers kappa_cut(m,a,b) > 0 and r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) >= 0, with r_cut defined on 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), such that if 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b) and 0 <= r <= r_cut(m,a,b,kappa), then X_cut is the unique minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk. Hence Delta_m^pm(r,kappa) = 0 and the best implementable design is attained exactly by P_cut on that low-robustness cell.
changed.to: Assume, in addition to ass:two-block-homophily and ass:positive-scale, the low-scale condition ass:low-scale-two-block, namely a+3b<2m. Put q=2(m-1), kappa_cut(m,a,b)=min{a-b, 2(m-b), ((2m-a-3b)(a-b)sqrt(q))/(a+b)}, and r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)=min{2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)), 2b(2m-2b-kappa)}. If 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b) and 0 <= r < r_cut(m,a,b,kappa), then X_cut is the unique minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk. Hence Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)=0 and the unique implementable covariance optimum is attained by P_cut. At the boundary r=r_cut uniqueness can fail when kappa=0, so the closed inequality in the frozen statement is too strong.
changed.reason: At kappa=0 and r equal to the supporting hyperplane threshold, the objective is linear along an exposed edge, so uniqueness can fail. Also a positive cut cell requires the graph-weight scale to be below the balance penalty scale; for example b>=m makes the all-ones vertex beat the cut vertex at r=kappa=0.

changed.from: There exists an explicit robustness frontier kappa_iid(m,a,b,r) with kappa_iid(m,a,b,r) >= kappa_cut(m,a,b) such that if kappa >= kappa_iid(m,a,b,r), then I_n is the unique minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk. Hence Delta_m^pm(r,kappa) = 0 and the best implementable design is attained exactly by the iid Rademacher law P_iid.
changed.to: Finite-kappa iid exactness holds only on the affine-balanced locus. More precisely, I_n is a minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk for some finite kappa if and only if a+3b=2m and r=2b(a+b) (equivalently r=4b(m-b)); on this locus, for every kappa>0, I_n is the unique minimizer and the implementable optimum is attained by P_iid. Off this locus, I_n is not a minimizer for any finite kappa, although minimizers converge to I_n as kappa tends to infinity.
changed.reason: I_n is an interior point of the block elliptope. The Frobenius penalty has zero tangential derivative there, so a finite-kappa optimum at I_n requires the linear part to be tangent-flat. This fails generically; large kappa only gives convergence toward I_n, not finite exactness.

changed.from: For every kappa with 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), there exist explicit frontiers r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa) such that for every r in (r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa), r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa)), there exists X in E_m^blk \ C_m^pm with F_{r,kappa}(X) < inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)). Equivalently, Delta_m^pm(r,kappa) > 0 throughout that interval.
changed.to: Assume ass:low-scale-two-block and ass:odd-community-size in addition to the frozen assumptions. With q=2(m-1) and kappa_cut as in the corrected cut theorem, define R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)=2b(a+b)(1+kappa/((a-b)sqrt(q))) and R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa)=(a+b)(2m-a-b-kappa/sqrt(q)). Define r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa)=(R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)+R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa))/2 and r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa)=R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa). Then for every 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa), and for every r in (r_gap^-,r_gap^+) the spread vertex X_spread in E_m^blk with spectral coordinates (m/(m-1),0,0) is the unique relaxed minimizer but is not plus-minus-one implementable. Consequently Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)>0 throughout that interval. The oddness restriction is necessary: if m is even, the whole block elliptope slice is implementable and the claimed positive gap is false.
changed.reason: The positive gap is impossible for even m because the spread, cut, and all-ones vertices are all plus-minus-one implementable and generate the whole block elliptope. The odd-m and low-scale restrictions are exactly what make the nonimplementable spread vertex exposed by the objective.

changed.from: Determine the exact critical frontier r_star(m,a,b,kappa) and the tight certificate rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa) that connect the low-kappa cut-exact cell, the high-kappa iid-exact cell, and the positive-gap strip into a single if-and-only-if phase description for Delta_m^pm(r,kappa).
changed.to: The exact if-and-only-if phase object is not a single scalar cut-break frontier with a high-kappa iid-exact cell. Instead, define the reduced elliptope triangle T_m={ (x,y,z): x>=0, y>=0, z>=0, 2(m-1)x+y+z=2m }, the parity threshold d_m=0 for even m and d_m=2/m for odd m, and the coefficient functional phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z)=c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(2(m-1)x^2+y^2+z^2), where c_x=2(m-1)((a+b)+r/(a+b)), c_y=2b+r/(2b), and c_z=2m. The tight certificate is rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa)=min_{(x,y,z) in T_m, y+z>=d_m} phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z)-min_{(x,y,z) in T_m} phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z). The exact zero-loss region is rho_star=0, equivalently the relaxed minimizer set intersects {y+z>=d_m}; the positive-gap region is rho_star>0. This gives the sharp implementability certificate, while the frozen high-kappa iid exact cell is replaced by the affine-balanced exceptional locus from thm:robust-corner-exactness.
changed.reason: The requested scalar boundary with a high-kappa iid-exact cell cannot exist because the robust iid cell is generically false. The exact object that remains is a reduced finite-dimensional convex certificate whose zero set is the if-and-only-if exactness region.

This coefficient separation is needed for a positive low-robustness cut cell followed by a nonempty spread-exposure interval; without it the spread vertex cannot be exposed before the all-ones direction becomes cheaper.

A strict plus-minus-one implementability gap in the two-parameter block slice requires the spread vertex to be infeasible; when m is even, assignments with zero sum in each community implement that vertex and the entire slice becomes implementable.

note: D0 maximality improvement: close rho_star (active-set SOCP) + assumption generalization
directive:
MAXIMALITY IMPROVEMENT (orchestrator directive — codex-adjudicated, faithfulness clean): CLOSE rho_star and upgrade oeq:sharp-phase-boundary from an open-ended question to a PROVED THEOREM giving the complete closed phase diagram. The remaining 'open' object is NOT field-open: minimizing phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z)=c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa*sqrt(q x^2+y^2+z^2) over the triangle T_m={x,y,z>=0, q x+y+z=2m} (q=2(m-1)) is a finite-dimensional active-set SOCP with a closed-form solution on each face. Derive it (construct-and-determine; do NOT hard-code constants — prove them):
(1) UNCONSTRAINED argmin over T_m: characterize by active set S subset {x,y,z}. Write alpha=(c_x/q, c_y, c_z) and beta=(1/q, 1, 1). First-order stationarity on a face with active (nonzero) set S gives a value lambda solving sum_{i in S}(lambda-alpha_i)^2/beta_i = kappa^2, with inactive coordinates j pinned to 0 requiring alpha_j >= lambda. Enumerate: the three vertices (cut (0,2m,0), spread (2m/q,0,0), all-ones (0,0,2m)), the three edge minimizers, and the interior minimizer. The three already-proven certificates (lem:cut-vertex-certificate, lem:spread-vertex-certificate, lem:frobenius-center-certificate) are the vertex/interior sub-cases; add the edge minimizers to complete the partition. Prove which active set is optimal as an explicit function of (alpha,beta,kappa).
(2) CONSTRAINED argmin over T_m intersect {y+z >= d_m} (d_m=0 even m, d_m=2/m odd m): equals the unconstrained solution when it already satisfies y+z>=d_m; else the minimizer lies on the truncation face y+z=d_m and is the explicit 1-D minimizer on that segment. Prove it.
(3) rho_star = (constrained value) - (unconstrained value): give PIECEWISE-CLOSED-FORM with explicit algebraic region boundaries; rho_star=0 exactly when the unconstrained argmin satisfies y+z>=d_m, else rho_star>0 equals segment-min minus unconstrained min. Upgrade oeq:sharp-phase-boundary to thm:sharp-rho-star stating this closed form; keep rho_star=Delta_m^pm as the proven identity.
(4) FRONTIER r_star: reframe as the explicit active-set CROSSING locus (where the unconstrained argmin crosses y+z=d_m) — a piecewise-algebraic frontier; give it explicitly.
(5) Add the high-r all-ones/z-vertex (0,0,2m) exact zero-loss cell as an implementable corner, completing the corner inventory.
ASSUMPTION CLEANUP (generalize under the SAME model — do NOT add any crux-encoding assumption; that is laundering): (a) ass:low-scale-two-block (a+3b<2m) is UNNECESSARY for the full closed rho_star theorem — retain it ONLY as a hypothesis of the low-kappa cut-window corollary, NOT of thm:sharp-rho-star. (b) Replace standalone ass:odd-community-size by the unified parity threshold d_m in the general theorem; retain oddness only where the STRICT positive gap is claimed. (c) ass:balanced-sign-design appears removable for the covariance-only objective because global sign symmetrization preserves X(P) and the objective depends on P only through X(P) — prove it and drop it for the stronger claim (strengthen-if-dischargeable); else keep it and note why.
Keep every currently-proven result intact (incremental — reuse unchanged proofs); ADD the closed-form rho_star theorem, edge-minimizer cases, all-ones corner, assumption generalizations. Do not weaken any statement to match a partial proof.

note: Upgrade oeq->thm:sharp-rho-star; close rho_star via active-set SOCP
directive: TARGET NODE UPGRADED: oeq:sharp-phase-boundary has been renamed to thm:sharp-rho-star (kind: theorem, status: to-prove) with a construct-and-determine statement asserting the CLOSED-FORM rho_star. Now PROVE thm:sharp-rho-star: derive the active-set SOCP solution of min phi over T_m in closed form (vertices, edges, interior; multiplier lambda solving sum_{i in S}(lambda-alpha_i)^2/beta_i=kappa^2 with alpha=(c_x/q,c_y,c_z), beta=(1/q,1,1)), the constrained (y+z>=d_m) minimizer as either X_rel or the closed-form segment-min on y+z=d_m, rho_star=constrained-unconstrained (=0 iff X_rel has y+z>=d_m), and the frontier r_star as the explicit crossing locus. Reuse lem:block-spectral-coordinates, lem:cut-vertex-certificate, lem:spread-vertex-certificate, lem:frobenius-center-certificate (the vertex/interior sub-cases) and ADD the edge-minimizer lemmas needed. Do NOT add any crux-encoding assumption (no low-scale, no standalone oddness — use d_m); this is a genuine closed-form derivation under the SAME model. Keep all other proven results intact.

Do NOT re-open or re-solve anything; all proofs are final and reused. Just reassemble the core from proto and re-render the note.

note: D0.5 referee findings: union-of-strata boundary + single-branch gap window + drop ass:positive-scale (definitional r>=0)
directive: D0.5-REFEREE FINDINGS ADDRESSED (narrow-toward-truth precision fixes so the D0.5 math panel CONVERGES; NO strengthening, tier stays field): (1) frontier_overclaim@thm:sharp-rho-star FIXED — clause (iv) now states the exactness boundary as the explicit FINITE UNION of supportwise algebraic strata over active sets S (not a single-valued frontier function / unique crossing); re-prove thm:sharp-rho-star accordingly, reusing lem:weighted-simplex-active-set / lem:weighted-simplex-truncation / lem:rounding-gap-reduction unchanged. (2) redundant_window_branches@thm:gap-window FIXED — thm:gap-window now uses ONLY the consumed single branches kappa_gap=((2m-a-3b)(a-b)sqrt(q))/(a+b) and r_cut_gap=2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)); re-prove using only these (thm:cut-corner-exactness keeps its full kappa_cut/r_cut min for the cut-vertex uniqueness certificate — unchanged). (3) unused_assumption@ass:positive-scale FIXED — ass:positive-scale DROPPED; r>=0 is now definitional (r=eta/gamma, eta>=0, gamma>0) recorded in the r symbol; theorem statements citing it now say 'the definitional domain r in [0,infty)'. Re-prove the affected theorems; every proof still holds because r>=0 is definitional and the cut/gap statements impose their own r-interval bounds. Reuse all unchanged lemma proofs; do NOT re-open or weaken anything else.

note: Comprehensive hypothesis tightening: cut-corner b<m (drop low-scale), remove redundant balanced-sign citations
directive: COMPREHENSIVE HYPOTHESIS-MINIMALISM TIGHTENING (break the D0.5 whack-a-mole; narrow-toward-truth only, no strengthening, no vacuous ranges, tier stays field): every theorem now states ONLY the hypotheses its own proof consumes. (1) thm:cut-corner-exactness: DROPPED ass:low-scale-two-block; replaced by the strictly WEAKER minimal non-vacuity side-condition b<m (low-scale a+3b<2m implies b<m, so this generalizes the theorem); kappa_cut trimmed to min{a-b, 2(m-b)} (the two branches the cut-vertex certificate consumes) — re-prove with b<m ensuring 0<=kappa<min{a-b,2(m-b)} is non-empty and lem:cut-vertex-certificate applies. (2) thm:gap-window: KEEPS ass:low-scale-two-block and ass:odd-community-size (genuinely consumed for kappa_gap>0 and parity); removed the redundant external ass:balanced-sign-design citation (sign symmetry is already built into P_m^sym / def:implementability-gap). (3) thm:sharp-rho-star and prop:symmetry-reduction: removed the redundant external ass:balanced-sign-design citation (encoded in def:balanced-design-class / P_m^bal which they reference); low-scale is NOT reintroduced — the cut stratum is bookkept via b<m. Re-prove the 4 changed nodes; reuse ALL lemma proofs unchanged; a minimalism referee re-reading must find ZERO redundant/unused hypothesis or min-branch on ANY node.

note: D0.5 FAIL fixes: even-m boundary, block-exchangeable dep, minimal cut-corner hyp
directive: FINAL D0.5-FAIL fixes (3 concrete findings, narrow-toward-truth): (1) false_boundary_characterization@thm:sharp-rho-star FIXED — clause (iv) now handles even m correctly: for even m, d_m=0 => whole triangle implementable => rho_star IDENTICALLY 0 and the exactness boundary is EMPTY; the nontrivial boundary (finite union of supportwise strata where y+z(X_rel)=2/m) exists ONLY for odd m. Clauses (i)-(iii) unchanged (hold for all m). (2) missing_dependency@thm:robust-corner-exactness FIXED — def:block-exchangeable-design-class added to depends_on (P_iid in P_m^sym). (3) redundant_assumption@thm:cut-corner-exactness FIXED — the explicit 0<=kappa<min{a-b,2(m-b)} clause DROPPED; the sole consumed hypothesis is 0<=r<r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) (which forces both cut-vertex-certificate branch bounds), with non-vacuity (kappa<min{a-b,2(m-b)}, needs b<m) stated only as a parenthetical remark. Re-prove the 3 changed theorems; reuse ALL lemma proofs unchanged; a strict minimalism/correctness referee must now find ZERO issues on any node.

The exact BOUNDARY/frontier r_star is NO LONGER claimed here — it is posed as the new oeq:sharp-frontier. Keep ass:two-block-homophily as the standing assumption (do not chase a>b minimality).
